title: The One With the Baby on the Bus

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: I once dated a Miss Crankypants. Lovely girl, kinda moody.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 약간 감정 기복이 있는 (kind of moody의 구어체 축약)
expressions.nuance: 'kinda'는 'kind of'를 빠르게 발음한 것으로, 특징을 살짝 완화해 부드럽게 표현할 때 쓴다. 직접적인 비판보다 가볍고 솔직하게 들린다.
expressions.example: She's fun to hang out with, kinda moody though. 예: 같이 있으면 재밌는데 좀 감정 기복이 있어.

expressions.dialogue: Monica: What if my own baby hates me? / Ross: Come on, don't do this.
expressions.speaker: Ross
expressions.nuance: 상대가 지나치게 걱정하거나 감정적으로 무너질 때 달래는 표현으로, 'Come on'이 앞에 붙어 답답함과 위로가 섞인 뉘앙스를 준다.
expressions.example: You're doing great. Come on, don't do this to yourself. 예: 잘하고 있잖아. 제발 혼자 그러지 마.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Come on, I don't really want to be doing this right now. I am carrying a very heavy couch.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh hey come on, don't-don't-don't do this! Umm, look let-let me tell you something, okay? Now when I watch you do a scene, I'm thinking, "Boy, she-she is a great actress!" (She's not buying it.) Uh but-but, I am also thinking, "She is hot!"

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Monica, will you stop? This is nuts.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 이건 말도 안 돼 / 너무 황당해
expressions.nuance: 'nuts'는 'crazy'의 구어 표현으로, 상황이 터무니없거나 지나칠 때 가볍게 쓴다. 격한 분노보다는 황당함·어이없음의 뉘앙스가 강하다.
expressions.example: We've been waiting three hours? This is nuts! 예: 세 시간을 기다렸다고? 이건 진짜 말도 안 돼!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: This is nuts. This is crazy. She came over for like two minutes, dropped off a fish tank, and left, end of story.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Okay sir, um-mm, let see if I got this right. Ah, so this is a half-caf, double tall, easy hazel nut, non-fat, no foam, with whip, extra hot latte, right? (the guy nods) Okay, great. (she starts to walk away and under her breath) You freak.

occ.speaker: Robert
occ.text: Oh God! Here we go again. Why does this keep happening to me? (spreads his legs) Is it something I'm putting out there? Is this my fault? Or am I just nuts?

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Quick, aim him at that pack o' babes over there.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.meaning: X를 Y 쪽으로 향하게 하다 / X를 Y에게 겨누다
expressions.nuance: 원래 무기나 카메라를 겨누는 표현인데, 여기서는 아기를 이성에게 어필하는 도구처럼 써서 유머를 만든다. 관심·자원·노력을 특정 방향으로 돌릴 때도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: Aim your best speaker at the investors in the front row. 예: 우리 발표 제일 잘하는 사람을 앞줄 투자자들한테 붙여봐.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: Maybe one of them will break away.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 그룹이나 집단에서 홀로 분리되어 나오는 동작을 표현한다. 연애 상황뿐 아니라 정치·스포츠에서도 '이탈·탈주'의 의미로 폭넓게 쓰인다.
expressions.example: One player broke away from the defense and scored. 예: 수비를 따돌리고 한 선수가 빠져나가 득점했다.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Why do you have to break up with her? Be a man, just stop calling.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: That's alright, I'll take it. Bad things happen to me anyway. This way I can break 'em up with a movie.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Ok. Well, before I say anything, I just need to know, is this one of those things where you break up with a guy, and then I tell you what I think, and then the next day you get back together with the guy, and I look like a complete idiot?

expressions.dialogue: Joey: He's got that great baby smell. Get a whiff of his head.
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 'whiff'는 바람결에 잠깐 스치는 냄새를 뜻하며, 비유적으로 '뭔가를 조금 눈치채다'라는 의미로도 자주 쓰인다.
expressions.example: If the boss gets a whiff of this plan, it's over. 예: 사장님이 이 계획을 조금이라도 눈치채면 끝장이야.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Hey-whoa, slow down. (Gets a whiff of him) No, keep moving. (Joey runs off.) Wow!

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Okay, Mike and Joey, get in position. Chandler, come with me. (they walk off, Ross looks down to Chappy, who he's holding and he gets a whiff of the dog's smell. He is clearly disgusted by it.)

expressions.dialogue: Caroline: I think my uterus just skipped a beat.
expressions.speaker: Caroline
expressions.nuance: 원래 'my heart skipped a beat'(심장이 쿵했다)라는 관용구인데, Caroline은 'uterus'(자궁)로 바꿔 모성 본능이 폭발했다는 유머를 만든다. 충격·설렘 두 상황 모두에 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: When she walked in, my heart skipped a beat. 예: 그녀가 들어오는 순간 심장이 쿵 내려앉았다.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: (to Chandler) What'd I tell you? What'd I tell you?
expressions.speaker: Joey
expressions.nuance: 자신의 예측이나 조언이 맞았을 때 의기양양하게 쓰는 수사적 표현으로, 실제로 대답을 기대하지 않는다.
expressions.example: She called back! What'd I tell you? 예: 그녀가 전화했잖아! 내가 뭐랬어?

expressions.dialogue: Caroline: What agency did you two go through?
expressions.speaker: Caroline
expressions.nuance: 어떤 공식 채널이나 절차를 이용했는지 물을 때 쓰는 표현으로, 'through'가 경로·수단을 뜻하는 전치사로 기능한다.
expressions.example: Which recruiter did you go through to get that job? 예: 그 직장은 어떤 헤드헌터를 통해서 구한 거야?

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: See, Rach, uh, see, I don't think that swearing off guys altogether is the answer. I really don't. I think that what you need is to develop a more sophisticated screening process.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Weekly Estimated Net Usage Systems. A processing term.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Ok Ross, just so you know, calling it a poopie diaper doesn't make this process any cuter.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Terry is a jerk, ok? That's why we're always saying 'Terry's a jerk!' That's where that came from.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 그게 그 말의 출처야 / 그래서 그런 말이 나온 거야
expressions.nuance: 어떤 말이나 관습의 유래를 설명할 때 쓰는 구어 표현으로, 약간 자기 합리화하거나 우스꽝스러운 상황에서도 자주 등장한다.
expressions.example: We always say 'measure twice, cut once'—that's where that came from after last year's mistake. 예: 우리가 '두 번 재고 한 번 자르라'고 하는 건 작년 실수에서 나온 말이야.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Done. / Phoebe: Really? / Rachel: Yeah. Who's workin' for you babe?
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 내가 다 해결해줬지? / 누가 너 편인지 알겠지?
expressions.nuance: 자신이 상대를 위해 뭔가를 성공적으로 처리했을 때 뿌듯함과 장난기를 섞어 쓰는 표현. 'Who's got your back?'과 비슷한 뉘앙스다.
expressions.example: I got us front-row seats. Who's workin' for you? 예: 앞줄 자리 구해놨어. 내가 챙겨주는 거 맞지?

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: I'm not some like sloppy second, charity band.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 남이 거절하고 남긴 것 / 찌꺼기 취급 / 2순위
expressions.nuance: 누군가가 먼저 쓰거나 거절한 것을 다음 사람이 받는 상황을 뜻하는 속어로, 피비는 전문 가수 다음에 들러리처럼 세워지는 것에 분개하며 이 표현을 쓴다.
expressions.example: I don't want to be sloppy seconds—if you wanted me, I should've been your first choice. 예: 2순위 취급은 싫어. 원했다면 처음부터 나를 선택했어야지.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: I put in the first two, just to, you know, get the ball rolling, and to make myself feel better.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.nuance: 무언가를 시작해 흐름을 만들어낸다는 뜻으로, 회의·프로젝트·모금 등 무언가를 '띄울 때' 폭넓게 쓰이는 관용구다.
expressions.example: Let's get the ball rolling with a quick brainstorm. 예: 간단한 브레인스토밍으로 일단 시작해보자.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Oh, that's good. Maybe he'll hear you and pull the cord.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 버스 정차 줄을 당기다 / (비유) 멈춤 신호를 보내다
expressions.nuance: 버스에서 내릴 때 줄을 당겨 정차를 알리는 행위로, 여기서는 아기 벤이 혼자 줄을 당겨줬으면 하는 블랙 유머로 쓰였다. 비유적으로 '무언가를 멈추게 하다'는 의미로도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: If things get too chaotic, just pull the cord and end the meeting. 예: 상황이 너무 혼란스러우면 그냥 멈추고 회의 끝내.

expressions.dialogue: Joey: We'll flip for it. Ducks or clowns. / Chandler: Oh, we're gonna flip for the baby?
expressions.speaker: Joey / Chandler
expressions.nuance: 'flip a coin'과 같은 의미로, 결정이 어려울 때 동전 뒤집기에 맡기자는 표현이다. 황당한 상황에 쓰여 웃음을 주기도 한다.
expressions.example: We can't decide between pizza or sushi—let's just flip for it. 예: 피자냐 초밥이냐 못 정하겠으면 그냥 동전 던지기로 하자.

occ.speaker: Susan
occ.text: I will flip you for it.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Oh we flipped for it. I got the cigar, he got the moustache. Figured if we both grew it, we'd look like dorks.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Right. Right. Well ah, y'know we could flip for it.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: All right, call it in the air. / Joey: Heads.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 동전이 공중에 있을 때 앞뒤를 외쳐 / 던지기 전에 선택을 선언해
expressions.nuance: 동전이 허공에 떠 있는 동안 heads(앞)·tails(뒤) 중 하나를 외치라는 뜻이다. 'call it'은 '예측을 선언하다'는 의미로도 쓰인다.
expressions.example: I'll flip—you call it in the air. Heads or tails? 예: 내가 던질게. 공중에 있을 때 외쳐. 앞면이야 뒷면이야?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: (looks at the ground and at Ross) I don't know Ross! I-I tell you what, let's flip to see who does it, okay? You-you call it in the air, all right?

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: Y'know, you called me that before so I-I took the liberty of fashioning a star out of aluminum foil. Now, no pressure, you like my cookies, you give me the star. (Hands it to her.)

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: What kind of scary-ass clowns came to your birthday?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: '-ass'는 미국 구어에서 형용사 뒤에 붙여 강조하는 접미사로, 'bad-ass', 'crazy-ass' 등에도 쓰인다. 비격식·속어이므로 공식 상황에는 쓰지 않는다.
expressions.example: That was one scary-ass horror movie. 예: 그 공포 영화 진짜 겁나게 무서웠어.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: I'm fine now, but it was really scary there for a while. I mean, someone slipped a-a threatening note under the door.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: It's a...tarantula! (Joey almost falls down from his drum-stool jumping up) Oh! God! Rachel, look, I'm sorry. What was I thinking giving Joey this big, gross, scary spider in such a poorly constructed cage?

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: Umm, now uh... It's a scary world out there, especially for a single mom. Y'know, now I always thought you and I had a special bond so... (He goes to one knee and pulls out a ring.) Phoebe Buffay, will you marry me?

expressions.dialogue: Terry: F.Y.I.. I've decided to pay a professional musician to play in here on Sunday afternoons.
expressions.speaker: Terry
expressions.nuance: 상대가 알아야 할 정보를 전달할 때 머리에 붙이는 표현으로, 이메일과 구어 모두에서 흔하다. 때로 약간 단호하거나 냉정한 뉘앙스를 준다.
expressions.example: F.Y.I., the deadline has been moved up to Friday. 예: 참고로, 마감이 금요일로 앞당겨졌어요.

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: Ok, ok, so you're not a fan, but I mean, come on, you cannot do this to her.
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.nuance: 직접 '싫다'고 말하는 대신 완곡하게 표현하는 방식으로, 음식·사람·아이디어 무엇에든 쓸 수 있다. 영미권에서 매우 일반적인 표현이다.
expressions.example: I'm not a fan of early morning meetings, honestly. 예: 솔직히 이른 아침 회의는 별로야.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Ah he's a, he's not a big fan of foosball.

occ.speaker: Amy
occ.text: Now listen, not that you guys could stop me or anything cause you know you'd be dead. I was thinking about changing her name. I'm just not really a big fan of Emily. [Transcriber note: I'm surprised that Rachel and Ross didn't say they weren't either here]

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Okay, not a fan of the tough love.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: I was looking forward to playing basketball, but I guess that's out the window.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.meaning: 그 계획은 물 건너갔어 / 날아가 버렸어
expressions.nuance: 원래 계획·기회·희망이 사라졌을 때 쓰는 관용구다. 'go out the window'로도 쓰며, 체념의 뉘앙스가 담긴다.
expressions.example: Once it started raining, our picnic plans went out the window. 예: 비가 내리기 시작하자 소풍 계획은 물 건너갔어.

occ.speaker: Ross
occ.text: Y'know, here's the thing. Even if I could get it together enough to- to ask a woman out,... who am I gonna ask? (He gazes out of the window.)

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: (looking out the window) Ew, ew, ew, ew ew ew ew ew!

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: Ok, I'm makin' a break for it, I'm goin' out the window.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: (to taxi driver) Just practicing. You're good. Carry on.
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 상대에게 하던 일을 계속하라고 허락하는 표현으로, 약간 권위 있거나 공식적인 느낌을 주기도 하고 여기처럼 어색한 상황을 넘기려 쓰기도 한다.
expressions.example: Sorry for the interruption—carry on with your presentation. 예: 방해해서 미안합니다. 발표 계속하세요.

occ.speaker: Robert
occ.text: Oh, no thanks. I always carry one in my sock.

occ.speaker: Joanna
occ.text: Oh yes, well there's the coffee too. (to the committee) Rachel can carry two things at once!

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: See look, women, carrying the bag. (He puts it on his shoulder and looks at himself in the mirror and likes what he sees.) But it is odd how a women's purse looks good on me, a man.

expressions.dialogue: Chandler: Do you know how long it's gonna be before you actually have to deal with this problem?
expressions.speaker: Chandler
expressions.nuance: 상황·사람·문제 등을 대처하거나 해결한다는 의미의 핵심 phrasal verb로, 긍정·부정 어느 맥락에서도 쓸 수 있다.
expressions.example: We'll deal with that issue after the launch. 예: 그 문제는 출시 이후에 처리하자.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Yes, 'cause now I have to go down there, and deal with them.

occ.speaker: Joey
occ.text: He's right, enough, already. What is the big deal about today? So you slept with her for the first time, so what? You slept with her for seven years after that.

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: So... What's the deal with umm, you and Joey?

expressions.dialogue: Monica: My brother has a slight phobia about needles.
expressions.speaker: Monica
expressions.nuance: 'slight phobia'처럼 'slight'을 붙이면 완화할 수 있다. 비이성적인 강한 두려움을 가리키지만, 일상에서는 '엄청 싫어한다'는 의미로 가볍게 쓰기도 한다.
expressions.example: I have a phobia about public speaking—my hands literally shake. 예: 나는 발표 공포증이 있어. 손이 진짜 떨려.

expressions.dialogue: Phoebe: I put in the first two, just to, you know, get the ball rolling, and to make myself feel better.
expressions.speaker: Phoebe
expressions.meaning: 먼저 돈을 넣다 / 씨앗 돈을 투입하다
expressions.nuance: 모금함·기타 케이스 등에 본인이 먼저 돈을 넣어 다른 사람의 참여를 유도하는 행동을 묘사한다. 비유적으로 일을 시작하기 위해 먼저 투자하는 상황에도 쓴다.
expressions.example: I'll put in ten dollars to get things started—who else is in? 예: 내가 먼저 만 원 낼게. 더 낼 사람 있어?

occ.speaker: Phoebe
occ.text: Oh no, don't hate, you don't want to put that out into the universe.

occ.speaker: Monica
occ.text: (puts down the lasagne) I just... can't do it.

occ.speaker: Chandler
occ.text: All right! I'm putting it out, I'm putting it out. (He drops it in Phoebe's coffee.)

expressions.dialogue: Rachel: if all you've ever actually had are healthy pets, then, whoosh!
expressions.speaker: Rachel
expressions.meaning: 이해 못 하고 그냥 지나치다 / 머리 위로 휙 날아가다
expressions.nuance: 무언가가 머리 위로 휙 날아가 버린다는 느낌으로, 농담이나 개념을 상대가 전혀 이해 못할 때 쓴다. 'It went right over my head'와 같은 맥락이며 제스처를 곁들이기도 한다.
expressions.example: I explained the joke but it just went whoosh—totally over her head. 예: 농담을 설명해줬는데 그냥 휙 지나가버렸어. 완전히 이해 못 하더라.

occ.speaker: Rachel
occ.text: Yeah, whoosh!

occ.speaker: Mike
occ.text: (releases a whoosh of air) Ya know, I'm going to take off.
